title: Mildly Spicy Korean Chicken Bowl
description: A yeasty, silky, weeknight Korean dish featuring pecorino-romano, robata-grill, and satvik-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Combine yeast and 1 cup water in a small bowl; let sit 5 minutes, or until frothy.
In a blender or food processor, combine chili flakes, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and 2 tablespoons water. Blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and pecorino-romano cheese (if using).
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough 5-7 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball. Flatten each ball slightly into a disk.
Grill the disks 2-3 minutes per side, or until charred and cooked through.
Transfer the grilled disks to a cutting board and let cool slightly.
Meanwhile, cook the chicken in a large skillet over medium-high heat 5-7 minutes, or until cooked through.
Slice the chicken into thin strips and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the sauce ingredients.
To assemble the bowls, place a grilled disk on each plate, top with chicken, roasted vegetables, green onions, and a sprinkle of sesame seeds (if using).
Drizzle the sauce over the top and serve immediately.
